loop_audit.last_stale_session_reap_note: CATCH-UP fire at 23:07 ET (post-daemon-crash-recovery). Context: daemon crashed at ~21:27 ET. Cron `*/30 watchdog-stale-session-reaper` continued firing but each fire failed with `prompt_async failed: fetch failed` because the daemon was down. State.json was NOT bumped between 17:30 ET (last successful fire) and 23:07 ET (this fire) - 11 missed ticks (18:00, 18:30, 19:00, 19:30, 20:00, 20:30, 21:00, 21:30, 22:00, 22:30, 23:00 ET). At 23:06:44 ET, daemon log shows `[stream-stall] recovery watchdog shipped OFF - no OpenCode turn will arm it` (recovery prep), then at 23:07:03 ET `[recovery] Found stale started sessions, recovering, count=8`. 8 sessions auto-recovered successfully (recovered=8, failed=0 at 23:07:54 ET): mvs_9d2e87f5f41a41a8af62d0c55ede0194 (mavis, me - this watchdog fire), mvs_03274aadb45341f6bb7c3bcad9047130 (b2-cyborg-it), mvs_e77b30371f6149dd8c38683d61847425 (b2-gl-people), mvs_b53a560888dc491594cb2dfddd37bf7f (b2-cyborg-it), mvs_e230b3d1f06248a79a5ea2f50ea85ee4 (b1-summers-ship), mvs_2622c9947976473f9c18331e8aedab9c (b1-summers-ship), mvs_1275be4cf85c43e280637667abfeb833 (b1-jerry-emyth), mvs_ef491344935741898750dd616ee1a8f5 (mavis/A0-Jumeau-Numerique-Amadeus). After recovery, daemon ran its own in-process stale-detector: 5 of the 8 recovered sessions were then `Emitting session.finish for finished session, reason=stale` and `Session marked as finished, reason=stale` (idleMs ranging 5826158-5921488 = 97-99min): mvs_03274aadb... (b2-cyborg-it), mvs_9d2e87f5... (mavis/me - but immediately re-resumed by cron redispatch), mvs_b53a560... (b2-cyborg-it), mvs_e77b303... (b2-gl-people), mvs_ef491344... (mavis/A0 - 23:08:44 idleMs=5921488). Plus mvs_1275be4cf... (b1-jerry-emyth) at 23:08:44 idleMs=58.8s after a real bash-tool call. Remaining 2 sessions continued working: mvs_e230b3d1... (b1-summers-ship daily-cycle-reset) and mvs_2622c994... (b1-summers-ship daily-cycle-reset both) - both resumed and still in flight at 23:11 ET writing weekly reviews. Watchdog decision: NO REAP this fire. All 8 crashed sessions already handled by daemon built-in stale-detector + recovery subsystem. The 21:30 fire (`mvs_89bb2d28f84f4d89a4aa2baf2be5a8fa`) is `status=error, message=prompt_async failed: fetch failed` (daemon was down at 21:30) - daemon will not auto-reap error sessions, only status=started. Awaiting human decision on whether to clean up the 21:30 error row. b3-xmen-people main `mvs_f766b86757c14fbd8070569d2cf71dae` was a false positive (39h idle) - `mavis session info` returns `status.type=finished`, list view showed stale snapshot. 0 FRICTION files emitted this fire. 0 `mavis session abort` API calls. 11 missed heartbeats consolidated into this single catch-up fire (checks 13 -> 25). Reap count stays at 5 (historical: 4 from 12:00 fire B2 daily-pulse cron batch + 1 from 15:00 fire Hawkeye zombie). Daemon auto-recovery is the primary defense for crash-related zombie sessions; watchdog cron is the secondary defense for in-flight hangs.
